findings.message: Tautological assertion: `assert s is s` is always true regardless of any Settings behavior. This test cannot fail and provides zero coverage of the code under test.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a meaningful assertion about Settings (e.g., assert a default field value, or assert that two Settings() instances produce expected attribute values).

findings.message: Over-mocked test: patches `Settings.resolved_log_level` (the very property under test) to return 'MOCKED', then asserts it equals 'MOCKED'. This only tests that `unittest.mock.patch.object` works; it does not exercise the real `resolved_log_level` logic and cannot detect any regression in it.
findings.suggestion: Remove the patch and test the real property by setting inputs it derives from (e.g., monkeypatch APP_LOG_LEVEL env var or set the underlying attribute) and assert the resolved value matches the documented resolution rules.
